steps: Day 0: Transfection of ExpiCHO-S cells
Sterile the plasmids by filtering through 0.22 µm spin-X column.

According to the transfection manual of ExpiFectamine™ CHO Transfection Kit, 0.8 µg of plasmid per ml of ExpiCHO-S cells (20 µg of heavy chain plasmid and 20 µg of light chain) were diluted in cold OptiPRO™ SFM in a 50 ml tube.Mix by swirling the tubes.
Gently invert the ExpiFectamine™ CHO Reagent bottle 4-5 times to mix.
Add respective volume of ExpiFectamine™ CHO Reagent to the tube of diluted DNA. Mix reaction by swirling the tube.
Incubate reaction at room temperature for at least 1 minute but no more than 5 minutes.

Take out the flask of ExpiCHO-S suspension cells that was prepared in step 3 out from the incubator andadd the transfection mix dropwise into the cells while swirling the flask gently during addition.
Place cells into back into incubator at 37°C with humidified atmosphere of 8% CO2 on an orbital shaker.
Day 1: Add ExpiFectamine™ CHO Enhancer and ExpiCHO Feed.
After 18-22 hours of transfection, add the required amount stated in of ExpiFectamine™ CHO Enhancer and ExpiCHO Feed.
Gently swirl the flask during addition.
Place cells into back into incubator at 37°C with humidified atmosphere of 8% CO2 on an orbital shaker for another 7 days before harvesting.
Day 8 : Harvest ExpiCHO-S cell culture supernatant
Collect transfected ExpiCHO-S suspension cells in 50 ml Falcon tubes.
Centrifuge the ExpiCHO-S suspension cells at 2000 rpm for 10 minutes.
Filter the supernatant through a 0.20 µm syringe filter to remove the cells and debris.
Add 10x PBS to neutralize pH of supernatant.
Day 9: Add Protein G agarose to the harvested ExpiCHO-S cell supernatant.
Wash 1ml of Protein G agarose (2 ml slurry) 3X with 5 ml of cell culture grade PBS in a 50 ml tube to remove storage buffer from Protein G agarose and add it into the supernatant.
Allow Protein G agarose to bind to the antibodies in supernatant overnight in 4°C or for 2 hours in room temperature with rotation before purification.
Day 10: Wash Protein G agarose and elute IgG antibodies
Centrifuge tube with supernatant and agarose at 150 g for 5 minutes.
Remove supernatant without disturbing the protein G agarose and collect in a new tube.
Wash 1 ml polypropylene column with cell culture grade water three times and with cell culture grade PBS for one time.
Transfer protein G agarose into column.
Wash protein G agarose with 3 column volume of cell culture grade PBS.
After washing protein G agarose with PBS, elute antibodies with 0.1 M of Glycine solution, pH 3.5 into 1.5 ml tubes with 200 µl of 1.0 M Tris, pH 8.0 to neutralise reaction.
Test 10 µl of various fractions of eluted antibody in 200 µl of 1X Bio-Rad Protein Assay Dye Reagent already prepared in a 96 well plate.
Pool the fractions of elute that turn blue in 1X Bio-Rad Protein Assay Dye Reagent together.
Day 10: Dialysis of antibodies

Transfer pooled fractions of elute into 10K MWCO Slide-A-Lyzer™ G2 Dialysis Cassettes and allow it to dialyse 3X, each time with 4 liters of 1x PBS prepared from 10X PBS. All buffer exchange should be done at 4°C with at least one of the dialysis done overnight.

Seed 50 ml of 6 x 10 6 cells/ml of ExpiCHO-S suspension cells in a 250 ml flask. Ensure percentage of viability is >95% before proceeding. Gently pipette cells into the medium and swirl flask to mix the cells. Put flask into incubator and prepare transfection mix.
Pre-warm ExpiCHO™ Expression Medium to 37°C in water bath.
Day 11: Collection of dialysed IgG antibodies
Collect dialysed antibodies from cassettes and concentrate to desired concentration using VIVASPIN 6 30,000 MWCO if necessary.
